17	export const MimeTypeContext = createContext<string[] | undefined>([])
18	
19	/** @public */
20	export function useDefaultHelpers() {
21		const editor = useMaybeEditor()
22		const { addToast, removeToast, clearToasts } = useToasts()
23		const { addDialog, clearDialogs, removeDialog } = useDialogs()
24	
25		const msg = useTranslation()
26		const printSelectionOrPages = usePrint()
27		const { cut, copy, paste } = useMenuClipboardEvents()
28		const copyAs = useCopyAs()
29		const exportAs = useExportAs()
30		const getEmbedDefinition = useGetEmbedDefinition()
31	
32		// This is the only one that will change during runtime
33		const breakpoint = useBreakpoint()
34		const isMobile = breakpoint < PORTRAIT_BREAKPOINT.TABLET_SM
35		const mimeTypes = useShallowArrayIdentity(useContext(MimeTypeContext))
36	
37		const insertMedia = useCallback(async () => {
38			if (!editor) return
39			const files = await getLocalFiles({
40				allowMultiple: true,
41				mimeTypes,
42			})
43			if (!files.length) return
44			editor.markHistoryStoppingPoint('insert media')
45			editor.putExternalContent({
46				type: 'files',
47				files,
48				point: editor.getViewportPageBounds().center,
49			})
50		}, [editor, mimeTypes])
51	
52		const replaceMedia = useCallback(
53			async (isImage: boolean) => {
54				if (!editor) return
55				const files = await getLocalFiles({
56					allowMultiple: false,
57					mimeTypes: mimeTypes?.filter((m) =>
58						isImage ? m.startsWith('image/') : m.startsWith('video/')
59					),
60				})
61				if (!files.length) return
62				const shape = editor.getOnlySelectedShape()
63				if (!shape || (isImage && shape.type !== 'image') || (!isImage && shape.type !== 'video'))
64					return
65	
66				editor.markHistoryStoppingPoint('replace media')
67	
68				const file = files[0]
69				editor.replaceExternalContent({
70					type: 'file-replace',
71					file,
72					shapeId: shape.id,
73					isImage,
74				})
75			},
76			[editor, mimeTypes]
77		)
78	
79		const replaceImage = useCallback(() => replaceMedia(true /* isImage */), [replaceMedia])
80		const replaceVideo = useCallback(() => replaceMedia(false /* isImage */), [replaceMedia])
81	
82		return useMemo(
83			() => ({
84				addToast,
85				removeToast,
86				clearToasts,
87				addDialog,
88				removeDialog,
89				clearDialogs,
90				msg,
91				isMobile,
92				insertMedia,
93				replaceImage,
94				replaceVideo,
95				printSelectionOrPages,
96				cut,
97				copy,
98				paste,
99				copyAs,
100				exportAs,
101				getEmbedDefinition,
102			}),
103			[
104				addToast,
105				removeToast,
106				clearToasts,
107				addDialog,
108				removeDialog,
109				clearDialogs,
110				msg,
111				isMobile,
112				insertMedia,
113				replaceImage,
114				replaceVideo,
115				printSelectionOrPages,
116				cut,
117				copy,
118				paste,
119				copyAs,
120				exportAs,
121				getEmbedDefinition,
122			]
123		)
124	}
125	
126	/** @public */
127	export type TLUiOverrideHelpers = ReturnType<typeof useDefaultHelpers>
128	
129	/** @public */
130	export interface TLUiOverrides {
131		actions?(
132			editor: Editor,
133			actions: TLUiActionsContextType,
134			helpers: TLUiOverrideHelpers
135		): TLUiActionsContextType
136		tools?(
137			editor: Editor,
138			tools: TLUiToolsContextType,
139			helpers: TLUiOverrideHelpers
140		): TLUiToolsContextType
141		translations?: TLUiTranslationProviderProps['overrides']
142	}
143	
144	export interface TLUiOverridesWithoutDefaults {
145		actions?: ActionsProviderProps['overrides']
146		tools?: TLUiToolsProviderProps['overrides']
147		translations?: TLUiTranslationProviderProps['overrides']
148	}
149	
150	export function mergeOverrides(
151		overrides: TLUiOverrides[],
152		defaultHelpers: TLUiOverrideHelpers
153	): TLUiOverridesWithoutDefaults {
154		const mergedTranslations: TLUiTranslationProviderProps['overrides'] = {}
155		for (const override of overrides) {
156			if (override.translations) {
157				for (const [key, value] of objectMapEntries(override.translations)) {
158					let strings = mergedTranslations[key]
159					if (!strings) {
160						strings = mergedTranslations[key] = {}
161					}
162					Object.assign(strings, value)
163				}
164			}
165		}
166		return {
167			actions: (editor, schema, helpers) => {
168				for (const override of overrides) {
169					if (override.actions) {
170						schema = override.actions(editor, schema, helpers)
171					}
172				}
173				return schema
174			},
175			tools: (editor, schema, helpers) => {
176				for (const override of overrides) {
177					if (override.tools) {
178						schema = override.tools(editor, schema, { ...defaultHelpers, ...helpers })
179					}
180				}
181				return schema
182			},
183			translations: mergedTranslations,
184		}
185	}
186	
187	function useShallowArrayEquality<T extends unknown[]>(array: T): T {
188		// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
189		return useMemo(() => array, array)
190	}
191	
192	/** @internal */
193	export function useMergedTranslationOverrides(
194		overrides?: TLUiOverrides[] | TLUiOverrides
195	): NonNullable<TLUiTranslationProviderProps['overrides']> {
196		const overridesArray = useShallowArrayEquality(
197			overrides == null ? [] : Array.isArray(overrides) ? overrides : [overrides]
198		)
199		return useMemo(() => {
200			const mergedTranslations: TLUiTranslationProviderProps['overrides'] = {}
201			for (const override of overridesArray) {
202				if (override.translations) {
203					for (const [key, value] of objectMapEntries(override.translations)) {
204						let strings = mergedTranslations[key]
205						if (!strings) {
206							strings = mergedTranslations[key] = {}
207						}
208						Object.assign(strings, value)
209					}
210				}
211			}
212			return mergedTranslations
213		}, [overridesArray])
214	}
215	
216	export function useMergedOverrides(
217		overrides?: TLUiOverrides[] | TLUiOverrides
218	): TLUiOverridesWithoutDefaults {
219		const defaultHelpers = useDefaultHelpers()
220		const overridesArray = useShallowArrayEquality(
221			overrides == null ? [] : Array.isArray(overrides) ? overrides : [overrides]
222		)
223		return useMemo(
224			() => mergeOverrides(overridesArray, defaultHelpers),
225			[overridesArray, defaultHelpers]
226		)
227	}

content.text:
I have enough context now. Let me present the implementation plan before writing code. --- ## Implementation Plan — MATT-133: Phase 5 Pin Tool ### Files to Create **1. `src/tools/pin/pin-shape.tsx`** — Red pushpin SVG component (24×32 px, tip at bottom-center of viewBox so the shape's bottom edge is the tip). **2. `src/tools/pin/pin-shape-util.ts`** — `ShapeUtil`: - `static type = 'pin'`, `static props = { attachedShapeIds: T.arrayOf(T.string) }` (using `T` re-exported from tldraw) - `TLPinShape = TLBaseShape<'pin', { attachedShapeIds: TLShapeId[] }>` - `getDefaultProps`, `canEdit = () => false`, `hideResizeHandles = () => true`, `isAspectRatioLocked = () => true` - `getGeometry`: `Rectangle2d` covering the pin head (top ~20×20 area) - `component` renders ``; `indicator` returns a `` around the head **3. `src/tools/pin/pin-tool.ts`** — `StateNode` (`static id = 'pin'`): - `onPointerDown`: get shapes at `inputs.currentPagePoint`, filter out pins, always create pin (shape `x = point.x - 12`, `y = point.y - 32` so tip sits on the cursor). All non-pin shape IDs go into `attachedShapeIds` regardless of count (0/1/2+). Wrap create + tool-switch in `editor.markHistoryStoppingPoint` + `editor.batch`, then `editor.setCurrentTool('select')`. **4. `src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts`** — React hook mounting the side effects: - **Module-level** `isPropagating` flag (per ticket). - Extract a pure `computePinUpdates(pins, movedShapeId, dx, dy)` helper → returns `{ shapeId, type, x, y }[]` list of updates. This is what unit tests drive. - `registerAfterChangeHandler('shape', (prev, next) => ...)`: skip if pin/no x-y change/propagating. Find pins whose `attachedShapeIds` contains `next.id`. Use helper to compute deltas. Set `isPropagating = true` inside try/finally around `editor.batch(() => editor.updateShapes(updates))`. - `registerAfterDeleteHandler('shape', (shape) => ...)`: skip pins. Find pins referencing `shape.id`; for each compute new set; if `< 2`, `editor.deleteShapes([pin.id])`, else `editor.updateShape` with pruned set. Wrap in `editor.batch`. - Cleanup disposes both handlers. **5. `src/tools/pin/pin-attachment.test.ts`** — vitest unit tests for `computePinUpdates`: - 2-shape set: moving A → returns single update for B (plus pin). - 3-shape set: moving B → returns updates for A, C (plus pin). - Overlapping sets `A={X,Y}, B={Y,Z}`: moving Y → returns updates for X, Z, plus pins A and B (no duplicate Y). ### Files to Modify **6. `src/components/canvas/editor.tsx`** — Wire it up: - Import `PinShapeUtil`, `PinTool`, `usePinAttachment`, `MapPin` from lucide. - Pass `shapeUtils={[PinShapeUtil]}`, `tools={[PinTool]}`, `overrides={uiOverrides}`, `components={{ Toolbar: PinToolbar }}` to ``. - `uiOverrides.tools` adds a `pin` entry with `icon:
` (TLUiIconJsx requires `ReactElement>`), `kbd: 'p'`, `label: 'Pin'`, `onSelect` → `editor.setCurrentTool('pin')`. - `PinToolbar` renders ``. - New `AttachmentBridge` component calls `usePinAttachment(editor)`; mounted inside the `EditorContext.Provider` after the editor is set. **7. `package.json`** — Add `vitest` as a devDependency + `"test": "vitest run"` script (required to run the unit tests the ticket asks for — CLAUDE.md notes no suite exists yet). ### Conventions - No semicolons, double quotes, 2-space indent (Biome). - `@/` path aliases; `cn()` unused here. - `"use client"` on React-using files. - Sort Tailwind classes manually. - Run `pnpm format` + `pnpm check` + `pnpm typecheck` before commit. ### Edge cases covered (from ticket) - 0/1 underlying shapes → pin still created, no grouping effect naturally falls out since `attachedShapeIds.length < 2` means no other shape to move in handler. - Delete shape → set shrinks; if `< 2`, pin is deleted (Decision 2). - 3+ overlapping → all attached (Decision 3). - Overlapping sets → handler guard + batch = single undo, no loop. ### Potential risks - `TLUiIconJsx` typing — if inline JSX doesn't match, fall back to registering a custom icon via `assetUrls` or a tiny wrapper div. - `afterChangeHandler` fires for every shape change including sub-millimeter nudges — comparing `prev.x === next.x && prev.y === next.y` is the early-exit. Shall I proceed with implementation?

5	 * Creates a validator for typed record IDs that ensures they follow the correct

18	 * const pageId = pageIdValidator.validate('page:main') // Returns 'page:main' as TLPageId
19	 *
20	 * // This would throw an error:
21	 * // shapeIdValidator.validate('page:abc123') // Error: shape ID must start with "shape:"
22	 * ```
23	 */
24	export function idValidator<Id extends RecordId<UnknownRecord>>(
25		prefix: Id['__type__']['typeName']
26	): T.Validator<Id> {

import type { RecordId, UnknownRecord } from '@tldraw/store'
import { T } from '@tldraw/validate'

/**
 * Creates a validator for typed record IDs that ensures they follow the correct
 * format with the specified prefix. Record IDs in tldraw follow the pattern
 * "prefix:identifier" where the prefix indicates the record type.
 *
 * @param prefix - The required prefix for the ID (e.g., 'shape', 'page', 'asset')
 * @returns A validator that checks the ID format and returns the typed ID
 * @public
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * const shapeIdValidator = idValidator<TLShapeId>('shape')
 * const validId = shapeIdValidator.validate('shape:abc123') // Returns 'shape:abc123' as TLShapeId
 *
 * const pageIdValidator = idValidator<TLPageId>('page')
 * const pageId = pageIdValidator.validate('page:main') // Returns 'page:main' as TLPageId
 *
 * // This would throw an error:
 * // shapeIdValidator.validate('page:abc123') // Error: shape ID must start with "shape:"
 * ```
 */
export function idValidator<Id extends RecordId<UnknownRecord>>(
	prefix: Id['__type__']['typeName']
): T.Validator<Id> {
	return T.string.refine((id) => {
		if (!id.startsWith(`${prefix}:`)) {
			throw new Error(`${prefix} ID must start with "${prefix}:"`)
		}
		return id as Id
	})
}

private _shouldIgnoreShapeLock = false

	/**
	 * Run a function in a transaction with optional options for context.
	 * You can use the options to change the way that history is treated
	 * or allow changes to locked shapes.
	 *
	 * @example
	 * ```ts
	 * // updating with
	 * editor.run(() => {
	 * 	editor.updateShape({ ...myShape, x: 100 })
	 * }, { history: "ignore" })
	 *
	 * // forcing changes / deletions for locked shapes
	 * editor.toggleLock([myShape])
	 * editor.run(() => {
	 * 	editor.updateShape({ ...myShape, x: 100 })
	 * 	editor.deleteShape(myShape)
	 * }, { ignoreShapeLock: true }, )
	 * ```
	 *
	 * @param fn - The callback function to run.
	 * @param opts - The options for the batch.
	 *
	 *
	 * @public
	 */
	run(fn: () => void, opts?: TLEditorRunOptions): this {
		const previousIgnoreShapeLock = this._shouldIgnoreShapeLock
		this._shouldIgnoreShapeLock = opts?.ignoreShapeLock ?? previousIgnoreShapeLock
		try {
			this.history.batch(fn, opts)
		} finally {
			this._shouldIgnoreShapeLock = previousIgnoreShapeLock
		}

		return this
	}

	/* --------------------- Errors --------------------- */

	/** @internal */
	annotateError(
		error: unknown,
		{
			origin,
			willCrashApp,
			tags,
			extras,

1	import classNames from 'classnames'
2	import { cloneElement, memo, ReactElement, useLayoutEffect, useRef } from 'react'
3	import { useAssetUrls } from '../../context/asset-urls'
4	import { TLUiIconType } from '../../icon-types'
5	
6	/** @public */
7	export type TLUiIconJsx = ReactElement<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>>
8	
9	/** @public */
10	export interface TLUiIconProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> {
11		icon: TLUiIconType | Exclude<string, TLUiIconType> | TLUiIconJsx
12		label: string
13		small?: boolean
14		color?: string
15		children?: undefined
16		invertIcon?: boolean
17		crossOrigin?: 'anonymous' | 'use-credentials'
18	}
19	
20	/** @public @react */
21	export const TldrawUiIcon = memo(function TldrawUiIcon({
22		label,
23		small,
24		invertIcon,
25		icon,
26		color,
27		className,
28		...props
29	}: TLUiIconProps) {
30		if (typeof icon === 'string') {
31			return (
32				<TldrawUIIconInner
33					label={label}
34					small={small}
35					invertIcon={invertIcon}
36					icon={icon}
37					color={color}
38					className={className}
39					{...props}
40				/>
41			)
42		}
43	
44		return cloneElement(icon, {
45			...props,
46			className: classNames({ 'tlui-icon__small': small }, className, icon.props.className),
47			'aria-label': label,
48			style: {
49				color,
50				transform: invertIcon ? 'scale(-1, 1)' : undefined,
51				...icon.props.style,
52			},
53		})
54	})
55	
56	function TldrawUIIconInner({
57		label,
58		small,
59		invertIcon,
60		icon,
61		color,
62		className,
63		...props
64	}: TLUiIconProps & { icon: TLUiIconType | Exclude<string, TLUiIconType> }) {
65		const assetUrls = useAssetUrls()
66		const asset = assetUrls.icons[icon as TLUiIconType] ?? assetUrls.icons['question-mark-circle']
67		const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
68	
69		useLayoutEffect(() => {
70			if (!asset) {
71				console.error(`Icon not found: ${icon}. Add it to the assetUrls.icons object.`)
72			}
73	
74			if (ref?.current) {
75				// HACK: Fix for <https://linear.app/tldraw/issue/TLD-1700/dragging-around-with-the-handtool-makes-lots-of-requests-for-icons>
76				// It seems that passing `WebkitMask` to react will cause a render on each call, no idea why... but this appears to be the fix.
77				// @ts-ignore
78				// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-deprecated
79				ref.current.style.webkitMask = `url(${asset}) center 100% / 100% no-repeat`
80			}
81		}, [ref, asset, icon])
82	
83		if (icon === 'none') {
84			return (
85				<div
86					className={classNames(
87						'tlui-icon tlui-icon__placeholder',
88						{ 'tlui-icon__small': small },
89						className
90					)}
91					{...props}
92				/>
93			)
94		}
95	
96		return (
97			<div
98				{...props}
99				ref={ref}
100				aria-label={label}
101				role="img"
102				className={classNames('tlui-icon', { 'tlui-icon__small': small }, className)}
103				style={{
104					color,
105					mask: `url(${asset}) center 100% / 100% no-repeat`,
106					transform: invertIcon ? 'scale(-1, 1)' : undefined,
107				}}
108			/>
109		)
110	}

// This allows us to include (or conditionally exclude or override) the default shapes in one go.
	//
	// In the `as` clause we are filtering out disabled shapes.
	[K in keyof TLGlobalShapePropsMap | TLDefaultShape['type'] as K extends TLDefaultShape['type']
		? // core shapes are always available and cannot be overridden so we just include them
			K extends 'group'
			? K
			: K extends keyof TLGlobalShapePropsMap
				? // if it extends a nullish value the user has disabled this shape type so we filter it out with never
					TLGlobalShapePropsMap[K] extends null | undefined
					? never
					: K
				: K
		: K]: K extends 'group'
		? // core shapes are always available and cannot be overridden so we just include them
			Extract<TLDefaultShape, { type: K }>
		: K extends TLDefaultShape['type']
			? // if it's a default shape type we need to check if it's been overridden
				K extends keyof TLGlobalShapePropsMap
				? // if it has been overriden then use the custom shape definition
					TLBaseShape<K, TLGlobalShapePropsMap[K]>
				: // if it has not been overriden then reuse existing type aliases for better type display
					Extract<TLDefaultShape, { type: K }>
			: // use the custom shape definition
				TLBaseShape<K, TLGlobalShapePropsMap[K & keyof TLGlobalShapePropsMap]>
}

import {
	RecordId,
	UnknownRecord,
	createMigrationIds,
	createRecordMigrationSequence,
	createRecordType,
} from '@tldraw/store'
import { mapObjectMapValues, uniqueId } from '@tldraw/utils'
import { T } from '@tldraw/validate'
import { SchemaPropsInfo } from '../createTLSchema'
import { TLPropsMigrations } from '../recordsWithProps'
import { TLArrowShape } from '../shapes/TLArrowShape'
import { TLBaseShape, createShapeValidator } from '../shapes/TLBaseShape'
import { TLBookmarkShape } from '../shapes/TLBookmarkShape'
import { TLDrawShape } from '../shapes/TLDrawShape'
import { TLEmbedShape } from '../shapes/TLEmbedShape'
import { TLFrameShape } from '../shapes/TLFrameShape'
import { TLGeoShape } from '../shapes/TLGeoShape'
import { TLGroupShape } from '../shapes/TLGroupShape'
import { TLHighlightShape } from '../shapes/TLHighlightShape'
import { TLImageShape } from '../shapes/TLImageShape'
import { TLLineShape } from '../shapes/TLLineShape'
import { TLNoteShape } from '../shapes/TLNoteShape'
import { TLTextShape } from '../shapes/TLTextShape'
import { TLVideoShape } from '../shapes/TLVideoShape'
import { StyleProp } from '../styles/StyleProp'
import { TLPageId } from './TLPage'

/**
 * The default set of shapes that are available in the editor.
 *
 * This union type represents all the built-in shape types supported by tldraw,
 * including arrows, bookmarks, drawings, embeds, frames, geometry shapes,
 * groups, images, lines, notes, text, videos, and highlights.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * // Check if a shape is a default shape type
 * function isDefaultShape(shape: TLShape): shape is TLDefaultShape {
 *   const defaultTypes = ['arrow', 'bookmark', 'draw', 'embed', 'frame', 'geo', 'group', 'image', 'line', 'note', 'text', 'video', 'highlight']
 *   return defaultTypes.includes(shape.type)
 * }
 * ```
 *
 * @public
 */
export type TLDefaultShape =
	| TLArrowShape
	| TLBookmarkShape
	| TLDrawShape
	| TLEmbedShape
	| TLFrameShape
	| TLGeoShape
	| TLGroupShape
	| TLImageShape
	| TLLineShape
	| TLNoteShape
	| TLTextShape
	| TLVideoShape
	| TLHighlightShape

/**
 * A type for a shape that is available in the editor but whose type is
 * unknown—either one of the editor's default shapes or else a custom shape.
 *
 * This is useful when working with shapes generically without knowing their specific type.
 * The shape type is a string and props are a generic object.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * // Handle any shape regardless of its specific type
 * function processUnknownShape(shape: TLUnknownShape) {
 *   console.log(`Processing shape of type: ${shape.type}`)
 *   console.log(`Position: (${shape.x}, ${shape.y})`)
 * }
 * ```
 *
 * @public
 */
export type TLUnknownShape = TLBaseShape<string, object>

/** @public */
// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-empty-object-type
export interface TLGlobalShapePropsMap {}

/** @public */
// prettier-ignore
export type TLIndexedShapes = {
	// We iterate over a union of augmented keys and default shape types.
	// This allows us to include (or conditionally exclude or override) the default shapes in one go.

1	import {
2	  HTMLContainer,
3	  type RecordProps,
4	  Rectangle2d,
5	  ShapeUtil,
6	  T,

1	"use client"
2	
3	import { PinShapeUtil } from "@/tools/pin/pin-shape-util"
4	import { PinTool } from "@/tools/pin/pin-tool"
5	import { usePinAttachment } from "@/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment"
6	import { createContext, useContext, useState } from "react"
7	import {
8	  DefaultToolbar,
9	  DefaultToolbarContent,
10	  type Editor,
11	  type TLComponents,
12	  type TLUiAssetUrlOverrides,
13	  type TLUiOverrides,
14	  Tldraw,
15	  TldrawUiMenuItem,
16	  useIsToolSelected,
17	  useTools,
18	} from "tldraw"
19	
20	// React Context is used so any descendant can call useEditor() without prop
21	// drilling. The value is null until tldraw fires onMount.
22	const EditorContext = createContext<Editor | null>(null)
23	
24	export function useEditor(): Editor | null {
25	  return useContext(EditorContext)
26	}

1	"use client"
2	
3	import { useEffect } from "react"
4	import type { Editor, TLShape, TLShapeId } from "tldraw"
5	import type { TLPinShape } from "./pin-shape-util"
6	
7	export interface PinSnapshot {
8	  id: TLShapeId
9	  x: number
10	  y: number
11	  attachedShapeIds: readonly TLShapeId[]
12	}
13	
14	export interface ShapeSnapshot {
15	  id: TLShapeId
16	  type: string
17	  x: number
18	  y: number
19	}
20	
21	export interface ShapeMove extends ShapeSnapshot {}
22	
23	// Module-level propagation guard. Lives outside React so it survives hook
24	// remounts and stays shared across any concurrent handler invocations inside
25	// a single editor batch.
26	let isPropagating = false
27	
28	// Pure helper so we can unit-test the delta math without mounting tldraw.
29	// Returns the list of shape/pin moves triggered by `movedShapeId` shifting by
30	// (dx, dy). The moved shape itself is not included — the caller has already
31	// applied that move. Each affected shape (and pin) is emitted once, even when
32	// several pins' attached sets overlap.
33	export function computePinUpdates(
34	  pins: readonly PinSnapshot[],
35	  movedShapeId: TLShapeId,
36	  dx: number,
37	  dy: number,
38	  getShape: (id: TLShapeId) => ShapeSnapshot | null
39	): ShapeMove[] {
40	  const updates: ShapeMove[] = []
41	  const seen = new Set<TLShapeId>([movedShapeId])
42	
43	  for (const pin of pins) {
44	    if (!pin.attachedShapeIds.includes(movedShapeId)) continue
45	
46	    if (!seen.has(pin.id)) {
47	      seen.add(pin.id)
48	      updates.push({
49	        id: pin.id,
50	        type: "pin",
51	        x: pin.x + dx,
52	        y: pin.y + dy,
53	      })
54	    }
55	
56	    for (const shapeId of pin.attachedShapeIds) {
57	      if (seen.has(shapeId)) continue
58	      const shape = getShape(shapeId)
59	      if (!shape) continue
60	      seen.add(shapeId)
61	      updates.push({
62	        id: shapeId,
63	        type: shape.type,
64	        x: shape.x + dx,
65	        y: shape.y + dy,
66	      })
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  return updates
71	}
72	
73	function snapshotPins(editor: Editor): PinSnapshot[] {
74	  const result: PinSnapshot[] = []
75	  for (const shape of editor.getCurrentPageShapes()) {
76	    if (shape.type !== "pin") continue
77	    const pin = shape as TLPinShape
78	    result.push({
79	      id: pin.id,
80	      x: pin.x,
81	      y: pin.y,
82	      attachedShapeIds: pin.props.attachedShapeIds,
83	    })
84	  }
85	  return result
86	}
87	
88	export function usePinAttachment(editor: Editor | null) {
89	  useEffect(() => {
90	    if (!editor) return
91	
92	    const disposeChange = editor.sideEffects.registerAfterChangeHandler(
93	      "shape",
94	      (prev, next) => {
95	        if (isPropagating) return
96	        if (next.type === "pin") return
97	        if (next.x === prev.x && next.y === prev.y) return
98	
99	        const pins = snapshotPins(editor)
100	        if (pins.length === 0) return
101	
102	        const dx = next.x - prev.x
103	        const dy = next.y - prev.y
104	
105	        const updates = computePinUpdates(pins, next.id, dx, dy, (id) => {
106	          const shape = editor.getShape(id)
107	          if (!shape) return null
108	          return { id: shape.id, type: shape.type, x: shape.x, y: shape.y }
109	        })
110	
111	        if (updates.length === 0) return
112	
113	        isPropagating = true
114	        try {
115	          editor.run(() => {
116	            editor.updateShapes(
117	              updates.map((u) => ({
118	                id: u.id,
119	                type: u.type,
120	                x: u.x,
121	                y: u.y,
122	              })) as Parameters<typeof editor.updateShapes>[0]
123	            )
124	          })
125	        } finally {
126	          isPropagating = false
127	        }
128	      }
129	    )
130	
131	    const disposeDelete = editor.sideEffects.registerAfterDeleteHandler(
132	      "shape",
133	      (deleted: TLShape) => {
134	        if (deleted.type === "pin") return
135	
136	        const pins = snapshotPins(editor).filter((pin) =>
137	          pin.attachedShapeIds.includes(deleted.id)
138	        )
139	        if (pins.length === 0) return
140	
141	        editor.run(() => {
142	          const pinsToDelete: TLShapeId[] = []
143	          const pinsToUpdate: Array<{
144	            id: TLShapeId
145	            type: "pin"
146	            props: { attachedShapeIds: TLShapeId[] }
147	          }> = []
148	
149	          for (const pin of pins) {
150	            const nextIds = pin.attachedShapeIds.filter(
151	              (id) => id !== deleted.id
152	            )
153	            if (nextIds.length < 2) {
154	              pinsToDelete.push(pin.id)
155	            } else {
156	              pinsToUpdate.push({
157	                id: pin.id,
158	                type: "pin",
159	                props: { attachedShapeIds: nextIds },
160	              })
161	            }
162	          }
163	
164	          if (pinsToUpdate.length > 0) {
165	            editor.updateShapes(pinsToUpdate)
166	          }
167	          if (pinsToDelete.length > 0) {
168	            editor.deleteShapes(pinsToDelete)
169	          }
170	        })
171	      }
172	    )
173	
174	    return () => {
175	      disposeChange()
176	      disposeDelete()
177	    }
178	  }, [editor])
179	}

1	import type { TLShapeId } from "tldraw"
2	import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest"
3	import {
4	  type PinSnapshot,
5	  type ShapeSnapshot,
6	  computePinUpdates,
7	} from "./use-pin-attachment"
8	
9	const id = (s: string) => `shape:${s}` as TLShapeId
10	
11	const xy = (shape: ShapeSnapshot | undefined) =>
12	  shape ? { x: shape.x, y: shape.y } : undefined
13	
14	function makeLookup(shapes: ShapeSnapshot[]) {
15	  const map = new Map(shapes.map((s) => [s.id, s]))
16	  return (lookupId: TLShapeId) => map.get(lookupId) ?? null
17	}
18	
19	describe("computePinUpdates", () => {
20	  it("moves the pin and the other attached shape when a 2-shape set moves", () => {
21	    const shapeA: ShapeSnapshot = { id: id("a"), type: "geo", x: 10, y: 10 }
22	    const shapeB: ShapeSnapshot = { id: id("b"), type: "geo", x: 50, y: 50 }
23	    const pin: PinSnapshot = {
24	      id: id("pin1"),
25	      x: 5,
26	      y: 5,
27	      attachedShapeIds: [shapeA.id, shapeB.id],
28	    }
29	
30	    const updates = computePinUpdates(
31	      [pin],
32	      shapeA.id,
33	      10,
34	      -5,
35	      makeLookup([shapeA, shapeB])
36	    )
37	
38	    expect(updates).toHaveLength(2)
39	    expect(xy(updates.find((u) => u.id === pin.id))).toEqual({ x: 15, y: 0 })
40	    expect(xy(updates.find((u) => u.id === shapeB.id))).toEqual({
41	      x: 60,
42	      y: 45,
43	    })
44	    // The moved shape itself should not be in the update list — the caller has
45	    // already applied that move.
46	    expect(updates.find((u) => u.id === shapeA.id)).toBeUndefined()
47	  })
48	
49	  it("moves every other member of a 3-shape set", () => {
50	    const shapes: ShapeSnapshot[] = [
51	      { id: id("a"), type: "geo", x: 0, y: 0 },
52	      { id: id("b"), type: "geo", x: 20, y: 20 },
53	      { id: id("c"), type: "image", x: 40, y: 40 },
54	    ]
55	    const pin: PinSnapshot = {
56	      id: id("pin1"),
57	      x: 0,
58	      y: 0,
59	      attachedShapeIds: shapes.map((s) => s.id),
60	    }
61	
62	    const updates = computePinUpdates([pin], id("b"), 5, 7, makeLookup(shapes))
63	
64	    expect(updates).toHaveLength(3)
65	    expect(xy(updates.find((u) => u.id === pin.id))).toEqual({ x: 5, y: 7 })
66	    expect(xy(updates.find((u) => u.id === id("a")))).toEqual({ x: 5, y: 7 })
67	    expect(xy(updates.find((u) => u.id === id("c")))).toEqual({ x: 45, y: 47 })
68	    // shape B is the mover and must not appear in the update list.
69	    expect(updates.find((u) => u.id === id("b"))).toBeUndefined()
70	
71	    // Preserve each shape's own type so the caller can round-trip the patch.
72	    expect(updates.find((u) => u.id === id("c"))?.type).toBe("image")
73	  })
74	
75	  it("propagates across overlapping sets without duplicating updates", () => {
76	    // A = {X, Y}, B = {Y, Z}. Moving Y must move X, Z and both pins — each
77	    // shape once, even though Y appears in both sets.
78	    const x: ShapeSnapshot = { id: id("x"), type: "geo", x: 100, y: 100 }
79	    const y: ShapeSnapshot = { id: id("y"), type: "geo", x: 200, y: 200 }
80	    const z: ShapeSnapshot = { id: id("z"), type: "geo", x: 300, y: 300 }
81	
82	    const pinA: PinSnapshot = {
83	      id: id("pinA"),
84	      x: 0,
85	      y: 0,
86	      attachedShapeIds: [x.id, y.id],
87	    }
88	    const pinB: PinSnapshot = {
89	      id: id("pinB"),
90	      x: 1,
91	      y: 1,
92	      attachedShapeIds: [y.id, z.id],
93	    }
94	
95	    const updates = computePinUpdates(
96	      [pinA, pinB],
97	      y.id,
98	      10,
99	      10,
100	      makeLookup([x, y, z])
101	    )
102	
103	    const ids = updates.map((u) => u.id)
104	    // Four distinct targets: two pins plus X and Z. No duplicates, no Y.
105	    expect(ids).toHaveLength(4)
106	    expect(new Set(ids).size).toBe(4)
107	    expect(ids).toContain(pinA.id)
108	    expect(ids).toContain(pinB.id)
109	    expect(ids).toContain(x.id)
110	    expect(ids).toContain(z.id)
111	    expect(ids).not.toContain(y.id)
112	
113	    expect(xy(updates.find((u) => u.id === x.id))).toEqual({ x: 110, y: 110 })
114	    expect(xy(updates.find((u) => u.id === z.id))).toEqual({ x: 310, y: 310 })
115	  })
116	
117	  it("returns nothing for a 1-shape attached set (no grouping effect)", () => {
118	    const shape: ShapeSnapshot = { id: id("a"), type: "geo", x: 0, y: 0 }
119	    const pin: PinSnapshot = {
120	      id: id("pin1"),
121	      x: 0,
122	      y: 0,
123	      attachedShapeIds: [shape.id],
124	    }
125	
126	    const updates = computePinUpdates(
127	      [pin],
128	      shape.id,
129	      10,
130	      10,
131	      makeLookup([shape])
132	    )
133	
134	    // Ticket decision: a pin over one (or zero) shape still exists but does
135	    // not group-move. The pin itself still follows, so we expect just the pin.
136	    expect(updates).toHaveLength(1)
137	    expect(updates[0]?.id).toBe(pin.id)
138	  })
139	
140	  it("ignores pins whose attached set does not contain the moved shape", () => {
141	    const shape: ShapeSnapshot = { id: id("a"), type: "geo", x: 0, y: 0 }
142	    const other: ShapeSnapshot = { id: id("b"), type: "geo", x: 0, y: 0 }
143	    const unrelated: PinSnapshot = {
144	      id: id("pin1"),
145	      x: 0,
146	      y: 0,
147	      attachedShapeIds: [id("c"), id("d")],
148	    }
149	
150	    const updates = computePinUpdates(
151	      [unrelated],
152	      shape.id,
153	      5,
154	      5,
155	      makeLookup([shape, other])
156	    )
157	
158	    expect(updates).toEqual([])

160	})

content.subject: Format, typecheck, commit, PR, review
content.description: Run pnpm format/check/typecheck/test; commit; push; open PR; run /review

content.subject: Create pin shape component + util

content.subject: Create pin StateNode tool
content.description: Write pin-tool.ts that creates the pin on pointer_down

content.subject: Create attachment side-effects hook + pure delta helper
content.description: Write use-pin-attachment.ts with computePinUpdates helper, isPropagating guard, delete handler

content.subject: Wire into editor.tsx (toolbar + mount hook)
content.description: Register shapeUtils, tools, overrides, components.Toolbar and mount usePinAttachment

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: phase 5 — pin tool (shape, toolbar, attachment side-effects)

- PinShapeUtil with non-editable, aspect-locked red-pushpin shape
- PinTool StateNode creates a pin at the pointer and records all
  non-pin shapes under it in props.attachedShapeIds, then returns
  to select tool
- usePinAttachment side-effect wires afterChangeHandler to propagate
  x/y deltas to every other shape in a pin's attached set (and the
  pin itself), batched under editor.run so undo is atomic and
  guarded by a module-level isPropagating flag
- afterDeleteHandler prunes deleted shape ids from every pin's set
  and deletes the pin when its set drops below 2
- DefaultToolbar override + assetUrls entry for the lucide MapPin
  icon gives the pin tool its slot in the toolbar
- vitest + unit tests for the delta math (2-shape, 3-shape, and
  overlapping-set propagation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: phase 5 — pin tool (shape, toolbar, attachment side-effects)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Implements the **pin** custom tldraw shape + `StateNode` tool + toolbar entry (lucide MapPin) so users can drop a pushpin over any set of shapes.
- Wires a `sideEffects` handler that propagates any attached shape's (x, y) delta to every other shape in a pin's attached set (and the pin itself), batched under `editor.run` so the whole group-move is a single undo entry. Protected by a module-level `isPropagating` guard — including for overlapping sets like `A={X,Y}, B={Y,Z}`.
- On shape delete, prunes the id from every pin's set; if the set drops below 2 the pin is also deleted (Decision 2 from the spec). Drop on 0/1 shapes still creates the pin with no grouping (Decision 3).
- Adds **vitest** + unit tests covering the delta math on 2-shape, 3-shape, overlapping-set, and 1-shape cases.

Linear: [MATT-133](https://linear.app/mattupham/issue/MATT-133/phase-5-pin-tool-shape-toolbar-attachment-side-effects)

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm typecheck` — passes
- [x] `pnpm check` — clean (Biome)
- [x] `pnpm test` — 5/5 passing (`computePinUpdates`)
- [x] `pnpm build` — production build succeeds
- [ ] Manual: load a PDF, drop a pin on 2+ overlapping shapes, drag one — whole group + pin move as one undo.
- [ ] Manual: delete shapes one at a time until set drops below 2 — pin auto-deletes.
- [ ] Manual: drop a pin on empty canvas / single shape — pin is created, no grouping effect.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

{"additions":1473,"author":{"id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNTc3OTY2","is_bot":false,"login":"mattupham","name":"Matt Upham"},"body":"## Summary
- Implements the **pin** custom tldraw shape + `StateNode` tool + toolbar entry (lucide MapPin) so users can drop a pushpin over any set of shapes.
- Wires a `sideEffects` handler that propagates any attached shape's (x, y) delta to every other shape in a pin's attached set (and the pin itself), batched under `editor.run` so the whole group-move is a single undo entry. Protected by a module-level `isPropagating` guard — including for overlapping sets like `A={X,Y}, B={Y,Z}`.
- On shape delete, prunes the id from every pin's set; if the set drops below 2 the pin is also deleted (Decision 2 from the spec). Drop on 0/1 shapes still creates the pin with no grouping (Decision 3).
- Adds **vitest** + unit tests covering the delta math on 2-shape, 3-shape, overlapping-set, and 1-shape cases.

Linear: [MATT-133](https://linear.app/mattupham/issue/MATT-133/phase-5-pin-tool-shape-toolbar-attachment-side-effects)

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm typecheck` — passes
- [x] `pnpm check` — clean (Biome)
- [x] `pnpm test` — 5/5 passing (`computePinUpdates`)
- [x] `pnpm build` — production build succeeds
- [ ] Manual: load a PDF, drop a pin on 2+ overlapping shapes, drag one — whole group + pin move as one undo.
- [ ] Manual: delete shapes one at a time until set drops below 2 — pin auto-deletes.
- [ ] Manual: drop a pin on empty canvas / single shape — pin is created, no grouping effect.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","changedFiles":9,"deletions":3,"state":"OPEN","title":"feat: phase 5 — pin tool (shape, toolbar, attachment side-effects)"}

+"use client"
+
+// A 24×32 pushpin drawn so the needle tip sits at x=12, y=32 — the bottom
+// center of the viewBox. The shape util anchors (x, y) to the top-left of the
+// bounding box, so we offset shape creation by (-12, -32) to land the tip on
+// the clicked point.
+export function PinShape() {
+  return (
+    <svg
+      width={24}
+      height={32}
+      viewBox="0 0 24 32"
+      fill="none"
+      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
+      aria-hidden="true"
+    >
+      <title>Pin</title>
+      <path
+        d="M12 2 C7 2 4 5 4 9 C4 13 8 14 9 18 L15 18 C16 14 20 13 20 9 C20 5 17 2 12 2 Z"
+        fill="#ef4444"
+        stroke="#991b1b"
+        strokeWidth={1.25}
+      />
+      <rect x={9} y={17} width={6} height={4} rx={1} fill="#7f1d1d" />
+      <path d="M12 21 L12 32" stroke="#1f2937" strokeWidth={1.5} />
+    </svg>
+  )
+}
diff --git a/src/tools/pin/pin-tool.ts b/src/tools/pin/pin-tool.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..d8ddb75
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/tools/pin/pin-tool.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+import { StateNode, type TLShapeId, createShapeId } from "tldraw"
+import { PIN_HEIGHT, PIN_WIDTH, type TLPinShape } from "./pin-shape-util"
+
+export class PinTool extends StateNode {
+  static override id = "pin"
+
+  override onEnter = () => {
+    this.editor.setCursor({ type: "cross", rotation: 0 })
+  }
+
+  override onPointerDown = () => {
+    const { editor } = this
+    const point = editor.inputs.getCurrentPagePoint()
+    const nonPinShapes = editor
+      .getShapesAtPoint(point)
+      .filter((shape) => shape.type !== "pin")
+
+    const attachedShapeIds: TLShapeId[] = nonPinShapes.map((shape) => shape.id)
+
+    editor.markHistoryStoppingPoint("create pin")
+    editor.createShape<TLPinShape>({
+      id: createShapeId(),
+      type: "pin",
+      x: point.x - PIN_WIDTH / 2,
+      y: point.y - PIN_HEIGHT,
+      props: { attachedShapeIds },
+    })
+    editor.setCurrentTool("select")
+  }
+}
diff --git a/src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts b/src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..877a4be
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,179 @@
+"use client"
+
+import { useEffect } from "react"
+import type { Editor, TLShape, TLShapeId } from "tldraw"
+import type { TLPinShape } from "./pin-shape-util"
+
+export interface PinSnapshot {
+  id: TLShapeId
+  x: number
+  y: number
+  attachedShapeIds: readonly TLShapeId[]
+}
+
+export interface ShapeSnapshot {
+  id: TLShapeId
+  type: string
+  x: number
+  y: number
+}
+
+export interface ShapeMove extends ShapeSnapshot {}
+
+// Module-level propagation guard. Lives outside React so it survives hook
+// remounts and stays shared across any concurrent handler invocations inside
+// a single editor batch.
+let isPropagating = false
+
+// Pure helper so we can unit-test the delta math without mounting tldraw.
+// Returns the list of shape/pin moves triggered by `movedShapeId` shifting by
+// (dx, dy). The moved shape itself is not included — the caller has already
+// applied that move. Each affected shape (and pin) is emitted once, even when
+// several pins' attached sets overlap.
+export function computePinUpdates(
+  pins: readonly PinSnapshot[],
+  movedShapeId: TLShapeId,
+  dx: number,
+  dy: number,
+  getShape: (id: TLShapeId) => ShapeSnapshot | null
+): ShapeMove[] {
+  const updates: ShapeMove[] = []
+  const seen = new Set<TLShapeId>([movedShapeId])
+
+  for (const pin of pins) {
+    if (!pin.attachedShapeIds.includes(movedShapeId)) continue
+
+    if (!seen.has(pin.id)) {
+      seen.add(pin.id)
+      updates.push({
+        id: pin.id,
+        type: "pin",
+        x: pin.x + dx,
+        y: pin.y + dy,
+      })
+    }
+
+    for (const shapeId of pin.attachedShapeIds) {
+      if (seen.has(shapeId)) continue
+      const shape = getShape(shapeId)
+      if (!shape) continue
+      seen.add(shapeId)
+      updates.push({
+        id: shapeId,
+        type: shape.type,
+        x: shape.x + dx,
+        y: shape.y + dy,
+      })
+    }
+  }
+
+  return updates
+}
+
+function snapshotPins(editor: Editor): PinSnapshot[] {
+  const result: PinSnapshot[] = []
+  for (const shape of editor.getCurrentPageShapes()) {
+    if (shape.type !== "pin") continue
+    const pin = shape as TLPinShape
+    result.push({
+      id: pin.id,
+      x: pin.x,
+      y: pin.y,
+      attachedShapeIds: pin.props.attachedShapeIds,
+    })
+  }
+  return result
+}
+
+export function usePinAttachment(editor: Editor | null) {
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (!editor) return
+
+    const disposeChange = editor.sideEffects.registerAfterChangeHandler(
+      "shape",
+      (prev, next) => {
+        if (isPropagating) return
+        if (next.type === "pin") return
+        if (next.x === prev.x && next.y === prev.y) return
+
+        const pins = snapshotPins(editor)
+        if (pins.length === 0) return
+
+        const dx = next.x - prev.x
+        const dy = next.y - prev.y
+
+        const updates = computePinUpdates(pins, next.id, dx, dy, (id) => {
+          const shape = editor.getShape(id)
+          if (!shape) return null
+          return { id: shape.id, type: shape.type, x: shape.x, y: shape.y }
+        })
+
+        if (updates.length === 0) return
+
+        isPropagating = true
+        try {
+          editor.run(() => {
+            editor.updateShapes(
+              updates.map((u) => ({
+                id: u.id,
+                type: u.type,
+                x: u.x,
+                y: u.y,
+              })) as Parameters<typeof editor.updateShapes>[0]
+            )
+          })
+        } finally {
+          isPropagating = false
+        }
+      }
+    )
+
+    const disposeDelete = editor.sideEffects.registerAfterDeleteHandler(
+      "shape",
+      (deleted: TLShape) => {
+        if (deleted.type === "pin") return
+
+        const pins = snapshotPins(editor).filter((pin) =>
+          pin.attachedShapeIds.includes(deleted.id)
+        )
+        if (pins.length === 0) return
+
+        editor.run(() => {
+          const pinsToDelete: TLShapeId[] = []
+          const pinsToUpdate: Array<{
+            id: TLShapeId
+            type: "pin"
+            props: { attachedShapeIds: TLShapeId[] }
+          }> = []
+
+          for (const pin of pins) {
+            const nextIds = pin.attachedShapeIds.filter(
+              (id) => id !== deleted.id
+            )
+            if (nextIds.length < 2) {
+              pinsToDelete.push(pin.id)
+            } else {
+              pinsToUpdate.push({
+                id: pin.id,
+                type: "pin",
+                props: { attachedShapeIds: nextIds },
+              })
+            }
+          }
+
+          if (pinsToUpdate.length > 0) {
+            editor.updateShapes(pinsToUpdate)
+          }
+          if (pinsToDelete.length > 0) {
+            editor.deleteShapes(pinsToDelete)
+          }
+        })
+      }
+    )
+
+    return () => {
+      disposeChange()
+      disposeDelete()
+    }
+  }, [editor])
+}

+      </HTMLContainer>
+    )
+  }
+
+  indicator() {
+    return (
+      <rect
+        x={PIN_HEAD_OFFSET}
+        y={0}
+        width={PIN_HEAD_SIZE}
+        height={PIN_HEAD_SIZE}
+      />
+    )
+  }
+}

+"use client"
+
+// A 24×32 pushpin drawn so the needle tip sits at x=12, y=32 — the bottom
+// center of the viewBox. The shape util anchors (x, y) to the top-left of the
+// bounding box, so we offset shape creation by (-12, -32) to land the tip on
+// the clicked point.
+export function PinShape() {
+  return (
+    <svg
+      width={24}
+      height={32}
+      viewBox="0 0 24 32"
+      fill="none"
+      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
+      aria-hidden="true"
+    >
+      <title>Pin</title>
+      <path
+        d="M12 2 C7 2 4 5 4 9 C4 13 8 14 9 18 L15 18 C16 14 20 13 20 9 C20 5 17 2 12 2 Z"
+        fill="#ef4444"
+        stroke="#991b1b"
+        strokeWidth={1.25}
+      />
+      <rect x={9} y={17} width={6} height={4} rx={1} fill="#7f1d1d" />
+      <path d="M12 21 L12 32" stroke="#1f2937" strokeWidth={1.5} />
+    </svg>
+  )
+}
diff --git a/src/tools/pin/pin-tool.ts b/src/tools/pin/pin-tool.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..d8ddb75
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/tools/pin/pin-tool.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+import { StateNode, type TLShapeId, createShapeId } from "tldraw"
+import { PIN_HEIGHT, PIN_WIDTH, type TLPinShape } from "./pin-shape-util"
+
+export class PinTool extends StateNode {
+  static override id = "pin"
+
+  override onEnter = () => {
+    this.editor.setCursor({ type: "cross", rotation: 0 })
+  }
+
+  override onPointerDown = () => {
+    const { editor } = this
+    const point = editor.inputs.getCurrentPagePoint()
+    const nonPinShapes = editor
+      .getShapesAtPoint(point)
+      .filter((shape) => shape.type !== "pin")
+
+    const attachedShapeIds: TLShapeId[] = nonPinShapes.map((shape) => shape.id)
+
+    editor.markHistoryStoppingPoint("create pin")
+    editor.createShape<TLPinShape>({
+      id: createShapeId(),
+      type: "pin",
+      x: point.x - PIN_WIDTH / 2,
+      y: point.y - PIN_HEIGHT,
+      props: { attachedShapeIds },
+    })
+    editor.setCurrentTool("select")
+  }
+}
diff --git a/src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts b/src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..877a4be
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/tools/pin/use-pin-attachment.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,179 @@
+"use client"
+
+import { useEffect } from "react"
+import type { Editor, TLShape, TLShapeId } from "tldraw"
+import type { TLPinShape } from "./pin-shape-util"
+
+export interface PinSnapshot {
+  id: TLShapeId
+  x: number
+  y: number
+  attachedShapeIds: readonly TLShapeId[]
+}
+
+export interface ShapeSnapshot {
+  id: TLShapeId
+  type: string
+  x: number
+  y: number
+}
+
+export interface ShapeMove extends ShapeSnapshot {}
+
+// Module-level propagation guard. Lives outside React so it survives hook
+// remounts and stays shared across any concurrent handler invocations inside
+// a single editor batch.
+let isPropagating = false
+
+// Pure helper so we can unit-test the delta math without mounting tldraw.
+// Returns the list of shape/pin moves triggered by `movedShapeId` shifting by
+// (dx, dy). The moved shape itself is not included — the caller has already
+// applied that move. Each affected shape (and pin) is emitted once, even when
+// several pins' attached sets overlap.
+export function computePinUpdates(
+  pins: readonly PinSnapshot[],
+  movedShapeId: TLShapeId,
+  dx: number,
+  dy: number,
+  getShape: (id: TLShapeId) => ShapeSnapshot | null
+): ShapeMove[] {
+  const updates: ShapeMove[] = []
+  const seen = new Set<TLShapeId>([movedShapeId])
+
+  for (const pin of pins) {
+    if (!pin.attachedShapeIds.includes(movedShapeId)) continue
+
+    if (!seen.has(pin.id)) {
+      seen.add(pin.id)
+      updates.push({
+        id: pin.id,
+        type: "pin",
+        x: pin.x + dx,
+        y: pin.y + dy,
+      })
+    }
+
+    for (const shapeId of pin.attachedShapeIds) {
+      if (seen.has(shapeId)) continue
+      const shape = getShape(shapeId)
+      if (!shape) continue
+      seen.add(shapeId)
+      updates.push({
+        id: shapeId,
+        type: shape.type,
+        x: shape.x + dx,
+        y: shape.y + dy,
+      })
+    }
+  }
+
+  return updates
+}
+
+function snapshotPins(editor: Editor): PinSnapshot[] {
+  const result: PinSnapshot[] = []
+  for (const shape of editor.getCurrentPageShapes()) {
+    if (shape.type !== "pin") continue
+    const pin = shape as TLPinShape
+    result.push({
+      id: pin.id,
+      x: pin.x,
+      y: pin.y,
+      attachedShapeIds: pin.props.attachedShapeIds,
+    })
+  }
+  return result
+}
+
+export function usePinAttachment(editor: Editor | null) {
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (!editor) return
+
+    const disposeChange = editor.sideEffects.registerAfterChangeHandler(
+      "shape",
+      (prev, next) => {
+        if (isPropagating) return
+        if (next.type === "pin") return
+        if (next.x === prev.x && next.y === prev.y) return
+
+        const pins = snapshotPins(editor)
+        if (pins.length === 0) return
+
+        const dx = next.x - prev.x
+        const dy = next.y - prev.y
+
+        const updates = computePinUpdates(pins, next.id, dx, dy, (id) => {
+          const shape = editor.getShape(id)
+          if (!shape) return null
+          return { id: shape.id, type: shape.type, x: shape.x, y: shape.y }
+        })
+
+        if (updates.length === 0) return
+
+        isPropagating = true
+        try {
+          editor.run(() => {
+            editor.updateShapes(
+              updates.map((u) => ({
+                id: u.id,
+                type: u.type,
+                x: u.x,
+                y: u.y,
+              })) as Parameters<typeof editor.updateShapes>[0]
+            )
+          })
+        } finally {
+          isPropagating = false
+        }
+      }
+    )
+
+    const disposeDelete = editor.sideEffects.registerAfterDeleteHandler(
+      "shape",
+      (deleted: TLShape) => {
+        if (deleted.type === "pin") return
+
+        const pins = snapshotPins(editor).filter((pin) =>
+          pin.attachedShapeIds.includes(deleted.id)
+        )
+        if (pins.length === 0) return
+
+        editor.run(() => {
+          const pinsToDelete: TLShapeId[] = []
+          const pinsToUpdate: Array<{
+            id: TLShapeId
+            type: "pin"
+            props: { attachedShapeIds: TLShapeId[] }
+          }> = []
+
+          for (const pin of pins) {
+            const nextIds = pin.attachedShapeIds.filter(
+              (id) => id !== deleted.id
+            )
+            if (nextIds.length < 2) {
+              pinsToDelete.push(pin.id)
+            } else {
+              pinsToUpdate.push({
+                id: pin.id,
+                type: "pin",
+                props: { attachedShapeIds: nextIds },
+              })
+            }
+          }
+
+          if (pinsToUpdate.length > 0) {
+            editor.updateShapes(pinsToUpdate)
+          }
+          if (pinsToDelete.length > 0) {
+            editor.deleteShapes(pinsToDelete)
+          }
+        })
+      }
+    )
+
+    return () => {
+      disposeChange()
+      disposeDelete()
+    }
+  }, [editor])
+}

content.text:
# Code Review — PR #6: Phase 5 Pin Tool

## Overview
Adds a custom tldraw pin tool: shape util, `StateNode`, toolbar slot (lucide MapPin icon), and `sideEffects`-based attachment propagation. All of MATT-133's acceptance criteria are implemented: pin creation, group-move as a single undo, auto-delete on set `< 2`, overlapping-set propagation. Includes vitest + 5 unit tests for the pure delta helper. Production build, typecheck, biome, and tests all green.

## Correctness ✅
- **Module augmentation** on `declare module "tldraw"` correctly teaches `TLShape` / `editor.createShape` about the `pin` type.
- **Module-level `isPropagating`** guard with `try/finally` — matches the ticket's explicit requirement and survives hook remounts.
- **`editor.run(...)`** (tldraw v4's rename of `batch`) makes the group-move atomic for undo.
- **`seen` set seeded with `movedShapeId`** correctly dedupes the pin and its sister shapes across overlapping attached sets like `A={X,Y}, B={Y,Z}`.
- **Delete handler** prunes ID from every pin's set, deletes pins whose set drops below 2 — matches Decision 2.
- **Early exits** (`isPropagating`, `type === "pin"`, `x/y unchanged`) keep the handler cheap on rotation/prop changes.

## Suggestions

### Nit 1 — Unnecessary `as` cast on `updateShapes`
`use-pin-attachment.ts:122` casts via `as Parameters<typeof editor.updateShapes>[0]`. Since the augmentation already taught `TLShape` about `"pin"`, tighten `ShapeSnapshot.type` from `string` to `TLShape["type"]` and the cast goes away:
```ts
export interface ShapeSnapshot {
  id: TLShapeId
  type: TLShape["type"]
  x: number
  y: number
}
```

### Nit 2 — Empty-extension interface
`export interface ShapeMove extends ShapeSnapshot {}` — prefer `export type ShapeMove = ShapeSnapshot`. Functionally identical; signals intent more clearly.

### Nit 3 — Missing test for stale-ID case
`computePinUpdates` handles `getShape` returning `null` (continues), but no test pins that behavior. Worth a sixth case to lock it in.

### Nit 4 — Inline SVG duplicates lucide's path
`editor.tsx:39–42` hand-copies the lucide MapPin path into a data URL. Ties the icon to a snapshot of lucide v1.8.0. Acceptable — tldraw's mask-based icon rendering needs a URL, not a JSX component — but worth a comment noting the source so a future maintainer knows where to refresh it from.

### Nit 5 — `useInterface` vs `type` for single-prop object
`TLPinShapeProps` is a one-field `interface`. `type TLPinShapeProps = { attachedShapeIds: TLShapeId[] }` is slightly more idiomatic for non-extensible prop objects. Purely stylistic.

## Risks
- **Augmenting `tldraw` module**: fragile if the package restructures its re-exports, but stable across tldraw v4.
- **`snapshotPins` scans all page shapes per change**: O(n) per event. Fine for typical canvases; revisit with an index only if profiling shows hot-path impact.
- **Dragging a pin directly**: `next.type === "pin"` early-exit means manually dragging a pin does NOT move its attached set. Ticket doesn't specify — acceptable — but worth confirming the intent.

## Tests & Conventions ✅
- 5 passing vitest unit tests covering ticket's required cases (2-shape, 3-shape, overlapping-set, termination).
- Biome clean, typecheck clean, production build succeeds.
- `"use client"` directives present, path aliases used, no `any`.

## Recommendation
**Approve with minor polish.** Implementation is correct and faithful to the ticket; the delta-math, recursion guard, and side-effect lifecycle all match the spec. Address the nits above for a tidier final form.

---

Now addressing the review feedback.

content.text:
All five steps of `/ticket` complete. Summary:

**PR:** https://github.com/mattupham/h2-frontend/pull/6 — feat: phase 5 — pin tool (shape, toolbar, attachment side-effects)

**What shipped**
- `PinShapeUtil` — non-editable, aspect-locked red pushpin, hit box around the head, tip anchored at the click point.
- `PinTool` — `StateNode` that creates a pin on `pointer_down`, records every non-pin shape at that point in `props.attachedShapeIds`, marks history, then returns to select.
- `usePinAttachment` — registers `afterChange` and `afterDelete` side-effects. Pure `computePinUpdates` helper carries all the delta math. Module-level `isPropagating` flag + `editor.run` guarantees atomic undo and no recursion across overlapping sets. Pin auto-deletes when its set drops below 2.
- Toolbar slot uses lucide `MapPin` via `assetUrls.icons`, pinned to the left of the default toolbar.
- vitest added; 6 unit tests cover 2-shape, 3-shape, overlapping sets, 1-shape (no-grouping), stale-id, and unrelated-pin cases.

**Review cycle**
- `/review` flagged 5 nits: removed an unnecessary `as` cast by tightening `ShapeSnapshot.type` to `TLShape["type"]`, flattened the empty-extension interface, converted `TLPinShapeProps` to a type alias, added a stale-id unit test, and sourced-annotated the inline MapPin data URL. All applied and pushed in commit `7ac0aff`.

**Verified:** `pnpm check`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm test` (6/6), `pnpm build` all pass.
